creator: gpalmer@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: feedback->analyzed

Whoops, wrong state last time. Sorry. 

There are 2 issues here. First, syslogd wasn't running. so 
when an attempt was made to send a signal to it, it couldn't 
find the PID file, producing the first 2 lines of error 
messages. Why syslogd died is not clear, and may not be an issue 
with more recent systems which are more stable. 

The second probably is a bug in makewhatis, in your case it seemed 
to think that it needed a /usr/X11R6/man. Can you still reproduce the 
problem on any more recent versions of FreeBSD?
